mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) exhibits a secular upward trend driven by technological progress and population growth. Rising industrial output expands corporate earnings and supports higher equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over any multi-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO has grown at a CAGR of approximately 2% over the past 50 years
failure_condition: INDPRO experiences a sustained decline of more than 5% year-over-year without a corresponding recovery within 12 months, or the rank IC over a rolling 3-year window becomes negative and remains so for two consecutive windows.

mechanism_narrative: As the unemployment rate approaches levels below the natural rate (estimated around 4.5%), labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, compressing corporate margins and prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, both of which depress equity prices over the subsequent month.
evidence_refs: fact:Wage growth historically accelerates when UNRATE falls below 4%
failure_condition: If UNRATE remains below 4% for 6 months but the S&P 500 continues to rise (positive returns in more than 60% of 20-day windows), or if wage growth measured by average hourly earnings fails to accelerate despite low unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed is tightening (rising FEDFUNDS) and housing starts (HOUST) begin to decline, it signals that higher rates are starting to bite into interest-sensitive sectors. This combination indicates an impending economic slowdown and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical episodes of tightening that caused housing downturns preceded market corrections (e.g., 2000, 2006)
failure_condition: If after a FEDFUNDS increase above 1.5% and a HOUST decline, the S&P 500 rallies by more than 5% over 20 days (i.e., false signal), or if the joint condition occurs but forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During the initial 12 months of a Federal Reserve rate hiking cycle, the first few rate increases are interpreted as a validation of economic strength. Higher rates at this early stage are associated with rising stock prices as investors anticipate robust earnings growth.
evidence_refs: fact:In 1994, 2004, and 2015-2016 hiking cycles, the first 12 months saw positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative returns over 5-day windows following any rate hike within the first 12 months of the cycle in more than 50% of instances, or if the correlation between FEDFUNDS change and forward returns is negative in that regime.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending, which drives corporate revenues and profits. When monthly retail sales growth exceeds a threshold of 0.5%, it signals accelerating demand and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for approximately 70% of GDP, and retail sales are a key indicator.
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth exceeds 0.5% but the S&P 500 declines by more than 2% over the next 20 days in more than 30% of occurrences, or if the signal's hit rate is below 55% over a rolling 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) confirm a broad-based expansion in consumer and investment demand. This dual confirmation of demand momentum supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month positive and HOUST month-over-month positive
evidence_refs: fact:Both series are leading demand indicators; joint positive readings historically precede strong GDP reports.
failure_condition: If joint positive readings are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 40% of cases, or if the combined signal has lower predictive power than each individually (tested via linear regression).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates rising factory output, often leading to higher corporate profits and positive sentiment. A month-over-month increase of more than 0.2% signals near-term momentum, which tends to push equity prices higher over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a coincident indicator; high-frequency changes are often followed by equity market reactions.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth above 0.2% leads to negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the response is not statistically significant (t-test).

mechanism_narrative: When the real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPI inflation) turns positive, monetary policy becomes restrictive. This tightening of financial conditions reduces corporate earnings expectations and raises discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Historically, periods of positive real rates have been associated with flat or declining equity markets (e.g., early 2000s, post-2015).
failure_condition: If real rates become positive but the S&P 500 continues to rise with a Sharpe ratio greater than 0.5 over 6-month periods, or if the signal's predictive rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
